skill.name: algorithmic-art
skill.description: Create reproducible generative art with p5.js, seeded randomness, interactive controls, and export-ready browser artifacts.
skill.summary: Build seeded p5.js generative art with controls, repeatable outputs, and PNG or SVG export guidance.
skill.author: Doyajin174

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution and weak cryptography patterns, but review found these are false positives from Markdown code fences and the phrase seeded randomness. The only retained concern is an external p5.js CDN script in the generated HTML template, which is a low-risk network dependency rather than exfiltration.

low_findings.title: External CDN Dependency in Generated HTML
low_findings.description: The HTML template loads p5.js from a public CDN. This is a legitimate creative-coding dependency, but generated artifacts depend on external network access and CDN integrity.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The external script URL is directly present in the template. No evidence shows secret collection, data exfiltration, or hidden network behavior.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Misclassified as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution locations are Markdown fenced examples and JavaScript snippets. They are not executable skill code and do not request shell access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line ranges are fenced Markdown blocks or ordinary p5.js examples. The skill allowlist includes file editing tools, not shell execution tools.

low_findings.title: Seeded Randomness Misclassified as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The description mentions seeded randomness for reproducible art. No evidence found of cryptographic use, password handling, tokens, encryption, or security-sensitive randomness.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is generative art reproducibility, not cryptography. The skill contains no code that uses seeded randomness for authentication or encryption.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Script Include
dangerous_patterns.description: Generated HTML includes a third-party p5.js script URL. Users should prefer pinned, trusted, or local library copies for offline or high-integrity workflows.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The CDN script include is visible in the HTML template. It is common for p5.js projects, but it is still a supply-chain dependency.

content.value_statement: Generative art can be hard to reproduce, tune, and present as a polished browser artifact. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to create p5.js artwork with seeded randomness, controls, and export options.
actual_capabilities: Creates self-contained HTML artifacts for p5.js generative art.
Uses seeded randomness so artworks can be regenerated consistently.
Defines sidebar controls for seeds, parameters, regeneration, reset, and download.
Provides reusable patterns for noise fields, particle systems, and parameter sliders.
Guides PNG and SVG export behavior for finished artwork.
Combines an artistic concept statement with implementation guidance.
limitations: Requires p5.js knowledge or generated JavaScript to produce the final artwork.
The template references a public CDN unless the library is replaced locally.
Does not include finished art assets or a complete viewer.html file.
Performance depends on the complexity of the generated sketch and target device.

use_cases.title: Build a Reproducible Art Sketch
use_cases.description: Create a p5.js artwork where every seed produces a stable visual result.

use_cases.title: Prototype Interactive Visual Systems
use_cases.description: Design controls for complexity, color, motion, and regeneration during visual exploration.

use_cases.title: Prepare Exportable Digital Artwork
use_cases.description: Generate browser-based artwork that can be saved as image output for review or publication.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Simple Sketch
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one reproducible visual idea.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a p5.js generative art sketch using seeded randomness. Use a simple visual theme, a sidebar seed control, and PNG download support.

prompt_templates.title: Add Parameter Controls
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a p5.js algorithmic art artifact with sliders for complexity, density, and color mode. Keep all random behavior tied to a seed.

prompt_templates.title: Develop an Art Movement Concept
prompt_templates.prompt: Invent a generative art movement, write a concise computational aesthetic statement, then implement the concept as an interactive p5.js artifact.

prompt_templates.title: Produce a Full Export Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a polished p5.js generative art viewer with seeded navigation, parameter controls, reset, regenerate, PNG download, and SVG export guidance.

output_examples.input: Create a seeded noise-field artwork with blue and gold accents.
output_examples.output: A browser artifact concept with a flow-field composition, seed controls, density and color sliders, and PNG download behavior.

output_examples.input: Make an interactive particle system for a digital exhibition study.
output_examples.output: A guided p5.js sketch plan with particle movement, deterministic regeneration, responsive canvas behavior, and sidebar controls.

output_examples.input: Turn a geometric meditation idea into generative art.
output_examples.output: A concise art statement plus implementation guidance for Fibonacci-inspired layout, parameter tuning, and repeatable seed navigation.

best_practices: Use explicit seeds for every random and noise operation.
Keep controls clear, bounded, and tied to meaningful visual parameters.
Test generated artifacts on different viewport sizes before sharing.
anti_patterns: Do not use unseeded randomness when reproducibility is required.
Do not overload the canvas with particles before checking performance.
Do not depend on remote libraries for offline or archived artwork.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It guides creation of p5.js generative art artifacts with seeded randomness, controls, and export behavior.

faq.question: Does it create finished images directly?
faq.answer: No. It helps create browser-based artwork that can then be rendered and exported.

faq.question: Why use seeded randomness?
faq.answer: Seeds make each generated artwork repeatable, so users can revisit, compare, and share exact results.

faq.question: Can it support interactive controls?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance includes sidebar controls for seeds, parameters, regeneration, reset, and downloads.

faq.question: Does it require network access?
faq.answer: The template references p5.js from a CDN. Users can replace it with a local library copy.

faq.question: Is this suitable for production galleries?
faq.answer: It can support prototypes and polished artifacts, but final work should be tested for performance and dependency integrity.
